emails.subject: Brothers Taco House — quick thought
emails.body:
Hey — noticed Brothers Taco House has 7,900+ Facebook fans but no real website behind all that love.

That's a huge audience with nowhere to land when they search for you on Google. A simple site with your menu and hours could turn those fans into daily customers — not just social ones.

Worth a 5-minute call to see if it makes sense?

— John
Jedaiflow.ai
emails.notes: Shares email with Frank's Grill — Brothers Taco House lead, prioritize this angle

emails.subject: Frank's Grill — catering idea
emails.body:
Hi — Frank's Grill has built a real institution in Houston. Multiple locations, loyal customers, decades in.

But there's no catering inquiry page on your site. Corporate catering in Houston is huge — a simple online form could bring in event contracts you're currently missing because there's no easy way to reach you.

Worth 5 minutes to talk through?

— John
Jedaiflow.ai
emails.notes: Same email as Brothers Taco House — send Brothers email first, hold this one to avoid double-hitting same inbox on day 1

emails.subject: Ostioneria Michoacan — quick thought
emails.body:
Hi — I noticed Ostioneria Michoacan is open until 2 AM every night but has no website. That's a lot of late-night searchers on Google who can't find you.

A single page with your hours, menu, and location could show up every time someone searches

— and drive real walk-ins.

Worth a quick call? I can show you what it would look like.

— John
Jedaiflow.ai
emails.notes: Phone-only lead. Call or find email via Google/FB before sending.

emails.subject: La Lupita — quick thought
emails.body:
Hi — La Lupita has 230+ photos on Yelp and a seriously loyal following. That's rare.

But there's no website — so when your fans try to share you or someone searches tacos near Pinemont, you're invisible. A simple site with your menu would fix that overnight.

Got 5 minutes for a call? I can walk you through what it would look like.

— John
Jedaiflow.ai
emails.notes: Phone-only lead. 230+ Yelp photos is strong social proof to mention on call.

emails.subject: Estela Mexican Grill — quick thought
emails.body:
Hi — Estela has an active Yelp presence and a great location on Airline Dr, but no standalone website.

Every day people search

and don't find you — even though you're right there. A simple site changes that.

Worth a 5-minute call to see what it could look like?

— John
Jedaiflow.ai
emails.notes: Phone-only lead. Breakfast/lunch angle strong for search traffic pitch.

emails.subject: Ostioneria Michoacan — quick thought
emails.body:
Hi — open until 2 AM with no website is a big missed opportunity. Anyone searching late-night seafood in North Houston can't find you online.

A one-page site with your menu, hours, and location would show up in every one of those searches. Takes a week to build.

Want to see what that could look like? Happy to do a quick call.

— John
Jedaiflow.ai
emails.notes: Phone-only lead. Same chain as N Fwy location — don't send same email, use this version.

emails.subject: Quick idea for Mariscos El Fabuloso
emails.body:
Hi — your site looks great but it's Spanish-only. Houston is bilingual and so is your customer base.

An English version with an online menu (and maybe even online ordering) could double your reachable audience without changing anything about your food or your vibe.

Worth a 5-minute call?

— John
Jedaiflow.ai
emails.notes: Has website but Spanish-only. Find contact email via site or Google Business.

emails.subject: Quick idea for El Dorado
emails.body:
Hi — El Dorado is open until midnight but your site doesn't have online ordering. A lot of late-night diners won't call ahead — they order online or they skip you.

Adding a simple ordering flow to your existing site (or building one fresh) could capture that late crowd automatically.

Got 5 minutes to talk through it?

— John
Jedaiflow.ai
emails.notes: Has Webador site, no ordering. Find contact email via site.

emails.subject: Quick idea for Puebla's
emails.body:
Hi — 362 Yelp reviews is serious social proof. That's real traffic landing on your page.

But your site is gallery-only — there's no way for that traffic to pre-order, book catering, or join a list. You're leaving money in visitors who click away.

Worth a quick call? Even one catering inquiry form could pay for itself fast.

— John
Jedaiflow.ai
emails.notes: 362 Yelp reviews is strong. Catering angle is the play here.

emails.subject: Nate's Diner — quick thought
emails.body:
Hi Nate — 247 Yelp photos and a loyal North Houston crowd is a real asset.

But there's no loyalty program or email list to keep them coming back. A simple "join our regulars" signup on your site could let you text or email your best customers before a slow Tuesday turns into an empty dining room.

Happy to show you what that looks like — 5 minutes?

— John
Jedaiflow.ai
emails.notes: Owner is Nate — use his name. Loyalty program angle differentiates from basic website pitch.

emails.subject: Quick idea for Jamaica Pon Di Road
emails.body:
Hi — 256 Yelp reviews and a growing catering business is impressive. But there's no automated way for people to request a catering quote on your site — so every inquiry requires a phone call.

A single catering form that collects event details and auto-responds could save hours a week and close jobs you'd otherwise miss.

Want to see how it works?

— John
Jedaiflow.ai
emails.notes: Catering intake is the specific angle. Find email via site or Google Business.

emails.subject: Quick idea for Doña Lena
emails.body:
Hi — Yelp top-100 in Houston is a serious accomplishment. You've got a massive audience already.

But there's no online ordering — so fans who don't want to wait in line have no option. A direct ordering page (not DoorDash, so you keep the margin) could turn your existing reputation into more daily revenue.

Worth 5 minutes?

— John
Jedaiflow.ai
emails.notes: Top 100 Yelp taco in Houston — strong anchor for pitch. Find email via site.
